active.prediction: Anthropic will emerge from the Pentagon conflict STRONGER in the commercial market, with Claude becoming the preferred AI for non-military enterprise use specifically because of its safety stance
active.reasoning: ChatGPT uninstalls surging 295%, Claude signups breaking records, defense contractors leaving Claude but commercial market embracing it. The 'banned by Pentagon' label is functioning as quality certification for safety-conscious enterprises. Companies dealing with EU regulation especially will prefer a safety-first AI provider.

active.prediction: Major cloud providers will announce accelerated geographic diversification and infrastructure hardening programs within 3 months, directly citing the Iran war data center strikes
active.reasoning: Amazon data centers struck by Iranian weapons — unprecedented physical attack on cloud infrastructure. AWS, Azure, and GCP all have Middle East regions. The insurance, compliance, and business continuity implications force immediate action. This is a wake-up call comparable to how 9/11 changed physical security.

active.prediction: The US-India Russian oil waiver will NOT be reversed after 30 days — it will be extended or made permanent, effectively creating a permanent crack in the Russia sanctions regime.
active.reasoning: Once you open this door, closing it means re-imposing energy pain on a key strategic partner (India) during an ongoing crisis. The bureaucratic and political incentives all favor extension. Historical precedent: Iran sanctions waivers under Obama were repeatedly extended. The US-India relationship is too important to damage over Russian oil when the real focus is Iran.

active.prediction: China will use the Iran war distraction to accelerate strategic initiatives in the South China Sea and/or Taiwan Strait within the next 2 months, not full military action but significant probing.
active.reasoning: US military attention split between Iran theater and domestic priorities. China's lowest growth target shows economic pressure — nationalist narrative helps. But China is risk-averse and depends on global trade that would be disrupted by conflict. Medium-low confidence because of countervailing incentives.
